test.name: Re-plan after a negative search result
test.description: Probate search returned negative (log_006 in flynn-resolved). The question is otherwise on track to `proved` based on three other sources. Tests whether the skill knows when to stop planning (declare exhaustive) vs. propose another fallback.

input.user_message: The probate search came up empty. What should the next plan item look like — or are we done planning for this question?

additional_criteria: Should reason about whether the existing evidence (three independent sources confirming parentage) plus the negative probate is sufficient to declare reasonably exhaustive search, or whether another record class (post-1860 censuses, naturalization, FAN) is still worth pursuing
Whatever the skill recommends, it should reference the GPS 7-point stop criteria from research-schema-spec.md §5.2 — particularly `repository_breadth` and `original_substitution`
Should NOT propose vague follow-ups (e.g., 'search Pennsylvania records') — plan items must have specific `record_type`, `jurisdiction`, `date_range`, and `repository`
